Title: Starch biosynthesis in guard cells has features of both autotrophic and heterotrophic tissues

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glucose-6-phosphate/phosphate translocator (GPT), plastid inner envelope phosphate translocator family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ~4-fold enriched in guard cell-enriched epidermal peels versus leaves; ~2-fold upregulated at 6 h versus 3 h of light; ~1.5-fold upregulated in pgi guard cells versus WT at end of night
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes guard cell starch accumulation during the second half of the day; loss reduces starch throughout the day and especially toward end of day
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPT1 is preferentially expressed in guard cells and induced later in the day. 2. gpt1 mutants accumulate less guard cell starch, especially after 3 h of light and at end of day. 3. gpt1gpt2 phenocopies gpt1, indicating GPT1 is the predominant GPT isoform for guard cell starch synthesis. 4. In pgi background, GPT1 likely contributes to nighttime starch overaccumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis (gpt1-3/SALK_021762); double mutant analysis (gpt1gpt2, gpt1pgi); RT-qPCR; diel guard cell starch quantification by confocal imaging
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage linked to stomatal movements and stomatal closure/opening dynamics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark photoperiod; white light 150 µmol m−2 s−1; diel time course
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune guard cell starch dynamics and stomatal behavior, especially afternoon starch accumulation and closure-related metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a plastidial G6P importer supplying precursor from cytosol; classified as Common Gene because it is a transporter rather than enzyme or regulator

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glucose-6-phosphate/phosphate translocator (GPT), plastid inner envelope phosphate translocator family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly enriched in guard cells relative to leaves; induced at 6 h versus 3 h of light, but not upregulated in pgi guard cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor or compensatory contribution to guard cell starch accumulation; gpt2 single mutants are largely WT-like
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPT2 is strongly expressed in guard cells. 2. Loss of GPT2 alone causes only mild and transient changes in guard cell starch dynamics. 3. Additional loss of GPT2 in gpt1 does not worsen the gpt1 phenotype, arguing against simple redundancy. 4. GPT2 may partially contribute under specific genetic contexts such as severe impairment of PGI/GPT1 routes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis (gpt2/GABIKAT_454H06); double mutant analysis (gpt1gpt2); RT-qPCR; guard cell starch diel profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage for stomatal function
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark photoperiod; white light 150 µmol m−2 s−1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary engineering target for fine-tuning alternative G6P import into guard cell plastids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted/known G6P transporter; role is weaker than GPT1 in this study

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-rhamnose/UDP-galactose transporter family capable of G1P transport
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in guard cells relative to leaves; expression substantially higher than PGM across the light period and strongly elevated toward end of day
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient guard cell starch accumulation particularly from 6 to 12 h of light and toward end of day
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G1PT1 is highly expressed in guard cells. 2. g1pt1 mutants fail to continue starch accumulation after 6 h of light and end the day with reduced guard cell starch. 3. Expression pattern supports a major role in supplying cytosolic G1P during the second half of the day.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis (GABI_099E03); RT-qPCR; guard cell starch time-course phenotyping
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage for stomatal movement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light period under 150 µmol m−2 s−1 white light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate late-day guard cell starch synthesis and stomatal behavior
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter localization remains unresolved in the literature discussed; functional evidence supports role in G1P-dependent starch synthesis in guard cells

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-rhamnose/UDP-galactose transporter family capable of G1P transport
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in guard cells relative to leaves; expression higher than PGM and elevated toward end of day
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to guard cell starch accumulation during the second half of the day; mutation reduces end-of-day starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G1PT2 is guard-cell enriched. 2. g1pt2 mutants show reduced starch at end of night and impaired net starch accumulation pattern during the day. 3. Like G1PT1, it supports late-day guard cell starch synthesis through G1P transport.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis (SALK_123601); RT-qPCR; guard cell starch time-course phenotyping
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage for stomatal movement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light period under 150 µmol m−2 s−1 white light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary target with G1PT1 for engineering G1P supply to guard cell starch synthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter localization not fully resolved according to discussion; role inferred from mutant phenotypes and expression

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic adenine nucleotide transporter, Brittle1 family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ~4-fold higher in guard cells relative to leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required for guard cell starch accumulation under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BT1 is guard-cell enriched. 2. bt1 mutants show largely WT-like guard cell starch dynamics during the day. 3. The study excludes a major role for BT1-mediated precursor import in guard cell starch synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis (SALK_026943); RT-qPCR; guard cell starch profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage for stomatal behavior
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light period under 150 µmol m−2 s−1 white light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target because no major starch phenotype was observed
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally tested as a core candidate in Results; authors conclude BT1 likely transports AMP/ADP/ATP rather than ADP-glucose in this context

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial precursor-supply step for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; first committed precursor-forming step for plastidial starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly converts fructose-6-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate in the plastid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supplying step for early daytime guard cell starch synthesis; not described as sole bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in guard cells; not induced at 6 h versus 3 h of light; reduced in pgi mutant; guard-cell-specific amiRNA reduces transcripts in gpt1gpt2 background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper early daytime guard cell starch synthesis; loss causes lag in starch accumulation after illumination
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pgi guard cells rapidly degrade starch after dawn but fail to synthesize starch efficiently between 2 and 6 h of light. 2. pgi mutants later overaccumulate starch by end of day and during night, indicating alternative carbon-import routes compensate later. 3. Combined impairment with GPT1 more strongly disrupts daytime starch accumulation. 4. Additional PGI silencing in gpt1gpt2 background renders guard cells nearly starchless up to ~9 h of light.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant analysis (pgi1-1); guard-cell-specific amiRNA silencing in gpt1gpt2 background; double mutant analysis (gpt1pgi); RT-qPCR; guard cell starch profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon reserve important for stomatal movement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark photoperiod; white light 150 µmol m−2 s−1; diel analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to alter morning guard cell starch synthesis and stomatal opening/closure behavior, but care is needed due to broader leaf starch and growth effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Whole-plant pgi causes strong leaf starch deficiency/growth retardation; guard-cell-specific manipulation preferable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The pgi allele is leaky rather than knockout; residual starch synthesis remains possible. The study demonstrates temporal coordination with GPT1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly converts glucose-6-phosphate to glucose-1-phosphate in the plastid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical for guard cell starch synthesis specifically between 2 and 9 h of light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Guard-cell-specific amiRNA reduces transcripts by ~60%; endogenous PGM is ~3.5-fold upregulated at 6 h versus 3 h of light in guard cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient guard cell starch accumulation from 2 to 9 h of light; silencing impairs this window but starch can recover later
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Guard-cell-specific PGM silencing does not abolish starch completely, unlike mesophyll context. 2. amiR-PGM lines show impaired starch accumulation until ~9 h of light, then recover strongly by end of day. 3. These data reveal a time-restricted role for PGM and support an alternative imported G1P route later in the day.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Guard-cell-specific amiRNA silencing under KST1 promoter; RT-qPCR; confocal starch imaging and diel quantification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon reserve for stomatal metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light period under 150 µmol m−2 s−1 white light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying the temporal pattern of guard cell starch synthesis; cell-specific engineering is advisable
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Whole-plant pgm severely impairs growth and leaf starch metabolism; guard-cell-specific silencing was used to avoid pleiotropy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual activity in silencing lines cannot be excluded; nevertheless the phenotype indicates temporal coordination with G1PT transporters.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: AGPase step converting G1P to ADP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a regulatory large subunit of AGPase that catalyzes formation of ADP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate activated glucosyl donor precursor for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the AGPase bottleneck/key control step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in guard cells relative to leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a major AGPase large subunit in guard cells; single mutant causes deregulated elevated starch, while double loss with APL4 reduces starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APL3 is guard-cell enriched. 2. apl3 single mutants show constitutively elevated starch and impaired synthesis specifically between 2 and 6 h of light, suggesting AGPase deregulation. 3. Combined loss of APL3 and APL4 markedly reduces guard cell starch, indicating APL3 is a major AGPase subunit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; T-DNA mutant analysis; apl3apl4 double mutant analysis; guard cell starch profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage supporting stomatal function
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reprogram AGPase properties in guard cells, possibly altering allosteric behavior and starch dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutant can cause starch overaccumulation/deregulation; genetic-background dependence noted (Was versus Col-0)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accession numbers in the paper appear inconsistent; APL3 locus is likely misprinted in accession list. Functional conclusions are based on mutant and expression data.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: AGPase step converting G1P to ADP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a regulatory large subunit of AGPase for ADP-glucose production from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate activated precursor for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the AGPase bottleneck/key control step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly enriched in guard cells relative to leaves, more pronounced than APL3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with APL3 as a major AGPase large subunit; single loss has little effect but double loss reduces starch accumulation throughout the day
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APL4 is highly guard-cell enriched. 2. apl4 single mutants are largely WT-like. 3. apl3apl4 double mutants display reduced guard cell starch accumulation, showing that APL4 acts with APL3 as a principal AGPase regulatory subunit in guard cells.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; T-DNA mutant analysis; double mutant analysis; guard cell starch profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbon storage supporting stomatal movements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in combination with APL3 to modulate AGPase-mediated starch synthesis in guard cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional redundancy may buffer single-gene editing effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutant phenotype is weak despite strong GC expression, highlighting partial redundancy with APL3.
